title: TC-DEL-001: Verify remaining device settings page opens correctly after deleting a powered-off paired device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair Device A (e.g., Poly Sync 20) and Device B (e.g., Poly Voyager 5200) to the phone.
Ensure Device A is powered ON and connected.
Power OFF Device B.
Navigate to Device Settings → tap Device B's entry.
Tap 'Delete Device' or 'Forget' to remove Device B from the paired list.
Confirm deletion when prompted.
Observe the device list — verify Device A is still listed.
Tap on Device A's entry to open its settings screen.
Verify Device A's name is displayed correctly (not showing Device B's name or garbled text).
Verify Device A's connection status shows 'Connected'.
Verify Device A's Audio Settings page is accessible and all settings (ANC, Transparency Mode, EQ, Volume) are displayed and interactive.
expected: After deleting powered-off Device B, Device A remains in the list with its correct name and 'Connected' status. Tapping Device A opens its full settings page with all audio settings (ANC, Transparency, EQ, Volume) accessible and functional — no UI corruption, blank screens, or incorrect device name displayed.

title: TC-DEL-002: Verify remaining device settings survive app background/foreground after deletion of a powered-off device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair Device A and Device B to the phone.
Ensure Device A is powered ON and connected.
Power OFF Device B.
Navigate to Device Settings → delete Device B (tap Device B → Delete Device → confirm).
Tap Device A to open its settings screen.
Verify Device A's name, connection status, and settings are correct.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds, then reopen Poly Studio Mobile from the app switcher.
Verify the app still shows Device A's settings screen (or navigates back to it).
Verify Device A's name is still correct and connection status shows 'Connected'.
Verify ANC, Transparency, EQ, and Volume sliders are still functional and show expected values.
expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding the app post-deletion, Device A's settings screen remains intact with correct name, 'Connected' status, and all audio settings functional — no UI corruption or data loss.

title: TC-DEL-003: Verify remaining device settings survive app force-kill and relaunch after deletion of a powered-off device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair Device A and Device B to the phone.
Configure Device A with specific settings: ANC level 70, Transparency Mode set to Speech with Free level 40, EQ set to Bass Boost, Volume at 60.
Ensure Device A is powered ON and connected.
Power OFF Device B.
Navigate to Device Settings → delete Device B (tap Device B → Delete Device → confirm).
Tap Device A to open its settings screen.
Verify Device A's name, connection status, and configured settings are displayed correctly.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Verify the app shows Device A in the device list with correct name and 'Connected' status.
Tap Device A to open its settings.
Verify ANC level is still 70.
Verify Transparency Mode is still set to Speech with Free level 40.
Verify EQ is still set to Bass Boost.
Verify Volume is still 60.
expected: After force-kill and relaunch, Device A retains its correct name, 'Connected' status, and all previously configured settings (ANC 70, Transparency Speech/Free 40, EQ Bass Boost, Volume 60) — no data loss or UI corruption from the deletion of Device B.

title: TC-DEL-004: Verify Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle after deleting a powered-off device does not corrupt remaining device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair Device A and Device B to the phone.
Ensure Device A is powered ON and connected.
Power OFF Device B.
Navigate to Device Settings → delete Device B (tap Device B → Delete Device → confirm).
Tap Device A to open its settings — verify correct name, status, and settings.
Power ON Device B (it was only powered off, not unpaired from phone Bluetooth).
Wait for Device B to reconnect to the phone.
Observe the Poly Studio Mobile app — verify how Device B is handled (re-added to list or shown as available for pairing).
Tap Device A's entry in the device list.
Verify Device A's name is still correct and connection status shows 'Connected'.
Verify ANC, Transparency, EQ, and Volume settings are still accessible and functional.
expected: When Device B is powered back on after deletion, the app handles the reconnection gracefully without corrupting Device A's state. Device A retains correct name, 'Connected' status, and all audio settings remain accessible and functional.

title: TC-DEL-005: Verify rapid deletion of one device followed by immediate navigation to another device's settings causes no UI corruption
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair three devices: Device A (Poly Sync 20), Device B (Poly Voyager 5200), Device C (Poly Studio P15) to the phone.
Ensure Device A is powered ON and connected.
Power OFF Device B.
Power OFF Device C.
Navigate to Device Settings.
Tap Device B → Delete Device → confirm deletion.
Immediately (within 1 second) tap Device A's entry to open its settings.
Verify Device A's name is displayed correctly.
Verify Device A's connection status shows 'Connected'.
Verify the Audio Settings page loads fully with ANC, Transparency, EQ, and Volume controls visible and interactive.
Navigate back to the device list.
Verify Device B is removed from the list.
Verify Device C is still listed (if it was previously paired).
Tap Device C's entry — verify its settings page opens correctly without corruption.
expected: Rapid navigation to Device A's settings immediately after deleting Device B does not cause UI corruption. Device A shows correct name, 'Connected' status, and all audio settings load fully. Device B is removed from the list. Device C remains listed and its settings are accessible without corruption.

title: TC-DEL-006: Verify sub-setting values (Transparency Environment/Speech) persist on remaining device after deleting a powered-off device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair Device A and Device B to the phone.
Ensure Device A is powered ON and connected.
Navigate to Device A → Audio Settings → Transparency Mode.
Set Transparency Mode to 'Environment' and set Surround level to 85.
Switch to 'Speech' sub-tab and set Free level to 60.
Navigate back to the device list.
Power OFF Device B.
Navigate to Device Settings → delete Device B (tap Device B → Delete Device → confirm).
Tap Device A to open its settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode.
Verify the active mode is still 'Environment' with Surround level at 85.
Switch to the 'Speech' sub-tab.
Verify Free level is still 60.
Switch back to 'Environment' sub-tab — verify Surround level is still 85.
expected: After deleting Device B, Device A's Transparency Mode sub-settings persist correctly: Environment mode with Surround level 85 and Speech mode with Free level 60. Switching between sub-tabs does not reset or corrupt values.

title: TC-DEL-007: Verify deleting a powered-off device while remaining device is in a call does not corrupt UI
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair Device A and Device B to the phone.
Ensure Device A is powered ON and connected.
Place a call using Device A (or receive an incoming call on Device A).
While the call is active on Device A, power OFF Device B.
Navigate to Device Settings → delete Device B (tap Device B → Delete Device → confirm).
While still on the call, tap Device A's entry to open its settings.
Verify Device A's name is displayed correctly.
Verify Device A's connection status shows 'In Call' or 'Connected' as appropriate.
Verify the Audio Settings page loads with ANC, Transparency, EQ, and Volume controls visible.
End the call.
Verify Device A returns to 'Connected' status and settings remain accessible.
expected: Deleting a powered-off device while the remaining device is in an active call does not corrupt the UI. Device A shows correct name, appropriate call/connection status, and all audio settings remain accessible during and after the call.
